payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right. To finish this up, let's see. I forgot to mention it last week, but those of you guys who have been playing the Telltale Batman game, the new Pact episode came out recently with Harley Quinn and Bane, and I play Mr. Freeze! It's been a lot of fun, and I've been continuously recording on it. Hope you guys enjoy more fun to come with that delightful character.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That's fair.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm taking over Gather Your Party on Friday nights now, and we're giving it a bit of a revamp. It's going to be a bit of a different vibe. It's going to be a little shorter. It's going to be fun. Our very first episode is going to be called Gather Your Goth, and we're going to be giving some of our friends goth makeovers and having a goth dance contest.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I get made over? When are you doing it?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Tomorrow night.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Shit, I'm not going to be here! Fuck, I really want to be made over!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Well I'll tell you what. You let me know, and you can come over and have a goth makeover.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Next time you do it, you do whatever it's called, and then in parentheses Laura Your Goth, or Goth Your Laura.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Gather Your Laura. You would goth out very well. I think I would be excited by that.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay. Unfortunately, she's been dead longer than a minute, so the spell does not take effect. You don't know how long it's been, or if perhaps your final moments of fucking with her was just enough to push it over the edge-- (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: And we're all on the ground now?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You are all now on the ground floor of Thar Amphala. As you glance around--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm flexing the wings out and around, and I'm making a feather bed with the ends of the wings on the ground and laying Velora down on it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Kash, tell me you have Revivify. Tell me you can bring her back.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: I believe I do.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I start combing her hair with my fingers, out of her eyes.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: Let me check. I do. Who am I casting it on?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Our sister.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: Oh. Oh, that doesn't look good. Yeah, okay. Let's see what we can do. I'm sorry, I'm still a little sore.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No. It's terrible. Please help her.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: It was a terrible fall. Give me one second.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: I'm so sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: Oh. Her? This one? Yeah, all right. Let's do that. I'm going to cast Revivify.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay. Unfortunately, she's been dead longer than a minute, so the spell does not take effect. You don't know how long it's been, or if perhaps your final moments of fucking with her was just enough to push it over the edge-- (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: And we're all on the ground now?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You are all now on the ground floor of Thar Amphala. As you glance around--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm flexing the wings out and around, and I'm making a feather bed with the ends of the wings on the ground and laying Velora down on it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Kash, tell me you have Revivify. Tell me you can bring her back.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: I believe I do.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I start combing her hair with my fingers, out of her eyes.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: Let me check. I do. Who am I casting it on?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Our sister.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: Oh. Oh, that doesn't look good. Yeah, okay. Let's see what we can do. I'm sorry, I'm still a little sore.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No. It's terrible. Please help her.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: It was a terrible fall. Give me one second.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: I'm so sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: Oh. Her? This one? Yeah, all right. Let's do that. I'm going to cast Revivify.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Raise Dead. You can bring someone back to life, yeah. It takes an hour to cast.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: It keeps happening! Sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: 61 seconds.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: I can speak to dead, if you'd like to talk with her.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No! I don't want to fucking talk to her, I want to have her back! (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: It's time for one message. Does she owe you money? I can have her find out where it is.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Do you have anything a little more potent right now?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I can raise dead.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: Well, there we go. See?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Is she going to come back as a zombie?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: She'll be a normal--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Just raised.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I might need some help. Where's Pike?

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: I can raise the dead, too, if you need help.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Pike has been slowly flying down.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: She doesn't come back as a zombie, right? It's raising dead.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Is it the actual Raise Dead spell?

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: Mine is.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It says three times a day.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: It takes you a little while, but thanks to Locate Object, you cruise through and you eventually find-- It's been smashed, but it's still intact and there you see the completely cut off, red-scaled, clawed hand of Arkhan the Cruel.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Is it an object? I guess it's inanimate now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It is considered an object now.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I do.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's good.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I got it. I've seen it. I have the spell slot.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. So you cast Locate Object and you have a very strong vision of the direction and it's maybe a hundred feet to your left, and it is under quite a bit of rubble.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Big man, come on. I'm going to need you to dig; it's over here.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Not a problem.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Rage dig.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Giving him direction. Rage-digging through, tossing veritable small boulders with one-handed scoops. You guys are having to dodge out of the way as they go flying by and skidding to a halt maybe 50, 60 feet behind him. Go ahead and make an investigation check with advantage, because of his aid.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That's about as good as it gets, because that's even worse. 16.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 16. Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: With a minus two.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: I'll say for the purposes of when you were helping with the ritual that he was casting, because that's an hour long, that would have been a short rest, easy.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Nothing.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We were talking about you. I mean, we were talking about-- sorry, I'm still working on it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, no, it's getting better. Should we get off of the titan, maybe?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It could fall.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Does anyone want to start walking towards the city to see how bad it really is?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We'll have to get through the titan, or we can fly down. Outside of that, I don't know.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: How many of us can fly? Well, I guess we could take a couple of trips on the carpet.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We've got a carpet. Some of us have wings.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: My wings are gone.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, your wings are gone. Pike's wings are gone. You guys have your broom; you have the carpet.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: So we can take three trips on the carpet, or something?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We can ride on the broom.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Have we taken a short rest, at this point?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, you guys would have taken a short rest, at this point.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: My beast shape's back.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Well, you used the scrying eye before you took the long rest in the Feywild, or was it after?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: He has pajamas he changes into.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: They're Batman pajamas.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Nothing.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Nothing? He's definitely recovering naked. Fuck! Nothing.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No pants?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No pants.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: He's in the buck!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You guys! He's shirt-cocking somewhere! (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He prepared! He knew! "It's the only way they won't find me!" He pulls his tearaways off and chucks them.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Just goes up and says hi to people.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Flashing innocents through all the city!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's his dominate action.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, right!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We're going to have to worry about him at some point.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Not now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I can try and scrying eye him. I also still have one more 5th-level spell.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, but how are we going to take him out if you use all your spells to find him?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I have a 6th.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Maybe we let him go.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: She's saying you got one more shot, and we still have some sunlight.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: One more time.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right. You see, as you guys are discussing, the Highbearer's eyes lock to Vax's, and you see softness return to his face. The tension, the unknowing, begins to subside with a smile, comes across his stilted, older elvish visage. "You all. There was a time I did not look past your beginnings. I am ashamed at my misjudgment." He looks around as folks begin to gather. He acknowledges J'mon, who's approached. They've bowed, carefully. "Many heroes rose to challenge this day. Many we've lost. And many others that remain to remember them. Let us vow to never forget those who gave everything so that we could still stand here to remember. You, Vox Machina, stand as that beacon of hope upon which we all we hold. You may not fully understand the scope of what you have done but I assure you, we do. And we will. We humble of Vasselheim are eternally in your debt. As is much of the ignorant world, perhaps. On behalf of Silver Talon's Reach, I thank you for everything." The rest of the room who's begun to gather now, you see other soldiers that were waiting outside as you first approached have begun to filter in. And as you glance back towards the entry hallway of the Platinum Sanctuary, you see faces. People that greeted you in. People hearing these words resounding in silence, and they all begin to bow low.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We're pretty sure he's alive and well. You know, he's a threat right now. (whispering) What, do we have proof?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, proof.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I've never known a true sense of security. I'm not entirely sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: The proof is we're here and we're alive.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: And that titan has stopped moving.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I am the champion of the Raven Queen. And she has allowed me to track his every movement for a while now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I recall you mentioning that when we last met."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: He's gone.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: There was a process. We had trammels.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You remembered the word.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Do I still have the remains of the broken trammel or did it shatter?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, that shit shattered. It turned to dust.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, glitter bomb. Oh, glitter-bomb Vecna! I like the idea that he got glitter-bombed.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "That was my good eye!" (laughter)

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: The sun-glass roof, which currently has been sitting within the shadow of the mountain, the sunlight begins to peak past and glide its way across the marble floor as everyone's cheering and the room now, which once was in shadow of the mountain, is now lit up with sunlight. Now you can see the glittering armor that peeks through the dirt, the grime, the blood, shining and sparkling on each of you, across Pike's beautiful armor, across all the warriors that stand here, across the Scalebearer's design, across your hair. The room is filled with light and though you sense the presence of probably Bahamut, glancing down you can't help but think that this is also the approving glance of Pelor, perhaps a bit of the Everlight. As the cheers begin to fade, the crowd begins to pull away. Conversations progress, thank-yous are given, people come up to you. And eventually you guys begin to step out of the Sanctuary and there you look down across the upper platform, looking down over the city. You can see the Birthheart, the Duskmeadow, pillars of smoke rising up as the sun now hangs high in the sky. A cold sensation takes you in the chest, Vax. A small pain. A second one. And your eyes shoot over across to the Raven's Crest, damaged but still standing. You turn around to look at the rest of your family and as you guys look at his face, in but a flash you can see this dark shape behind him. This looming black cloak, this lithe dress that cascades down into a bell and drags across the ground. And there you see the form of the Raven Queen, porcelain mask, long dark hair behind, as her hands slowly creep over the shoulders of Vax'ildan.

preceding_turns.speaker: ALL
preceding_turns.text: Huzzah! (cheering)

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Hey, can you hear me?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: With a wave of cheers that roar from within these marbled halls that echo, boom and reverberate--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: To 1,000 years of Vasselheim! Huzzah!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Cheers and cries and fists raise and Pike's cheering--

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yay! (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You see Velora pull away from Allura's arm, who's come by to help you carry her, and she: (weakly) "Yay." And Allura looks to you with an absolute look of respect and pride. Even the Highbearer seems to be taken by the words. Shit, everyone in the room gets temporary hit points, practically.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah! 20 hit points now.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: I'll allow you one more strength check with disadvantage.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I mean, it's been a hard day. I think we all need a really good bowel movement.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I start walking out of the Sanctuary.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Pike, make a strength check.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: No. Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So roll and add your strength modifier, which I think is four.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: It's five.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh, it's five.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: She's got the gauntlets on.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Gauntlets bring it to 19, so it'll be a plus four, I'm pretty sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Wait, I think I had a plus one somewhere.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Did you get some boon or boost or something?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: With a plus four, it's 17.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 17. Grog, roll a strength check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Roll a one! Roll a one!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 19. Pike, your feet begin to scrape against the marble as Grog's form and strength, which is just a little bit more than you. He's a little more monster than your monster right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Can I swing my other hand up and try to pull more?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: She looks around at the rest of you, pats you on the head for a minute and pulls away and goes, "You get to wear the greatest outfits, don't you?"

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: It's so wonderful to see your face.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "We've housed who we can. We're a bit at capacity at the moment."

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Well, it looks a bit better than I thought it would.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "We did our best to defend. Is it done?"

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: It's done.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You see her eyes crack at that moment and tears begin to flow. She smiles. "I knew you could do it."

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Well, we all did.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "I don't really drink. But after a day like today, I think I need one."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Maybe a message of Sending. Can anyone do that? Pike? Do you have the message of Sending?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: To whom?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: To my father.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: To let him know that his daughter is still alive.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I don't have it with me, but I--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Allura, you've done it before.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yes."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Could you send something to my father?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I could. I could also bring her there, if you'd like."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No. Velora, would you want to go home right now?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Right now she's holding onto your neck a bit. She looks at you. "I miss you."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I miss you too.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Can I stay a bit longer?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Okay."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I think that's a great idea.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I think so too. Allura, will you say that Velora is safe, and that I'll be paying a visit very soon to return her?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I believe that," and for a moment she looks just beyond your eyes, squints, "he's aware."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "So to drinks?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: To drinks.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Kima, as she walks in, sauntering in, with her blade being sheathed in the back of her giant scabbard, her Holy Avenger. "Got your message, honey! Not going to pass up a drink after a day like this!" (cracking) "Ow, ow, ow!" She comes and sits down next to Allura and puts her arm around her real fast. "This is new!" and points to the drink in front of Allura.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "But of course, sir! My pleasure!" Takes the coin.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: If everyone's all right with that.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Of course, dear.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Can I also start pulling out the remaining eight expensive-ass bottles of wine from the bag of holding?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Hell yeah! Drunk as shit! Now's the time!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Within moments, your table is covered in terrible beer and really expensive wine!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Expensive-ass!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's what you wrote down, too.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Expensive-ass bottles of wine.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You pull two tables together and you guys are in the process-- You're a fair bit into your cups, and then the somewhat ajar door on the exterior slams open as the light filters in from the outside, and there you see standing the general half-height of an armored halfling, partially bloodied, armor dented as shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Kima!

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: 15. Something creeping in the back of your mind, something you've learned, something you've heard, really tells you that this is powerful and chaotic.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Nothing. You're fine. Love you very much.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I've lost the love of my life today. I'm allowed to be belligerent.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You are.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Are we throwing caution to the wind?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I do have something that might up the party a little bit.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay, what is it?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I reach into the bag of holding and I pull out a little leather pouch.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Do you have that sandkheg shit?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What is that? What have you got?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: So I've got this little deck of cards and every time someone pulls from it, great shit happens.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I'm in.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: One. Two. Scanlan?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I've seen this before, haven't I? I think I was with you when you got the guy rich. Wasn't I?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, he was on his own.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: He's the Lord of the Quadroads now.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I was with him when he did one of them, though.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You're semi-aware that there's something going on. Make an arcana check.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh, this could go so badly.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You watch for a brief second her tough exterior break, and you can see her eyes welling up a little bit. She looks at the ground for a minute, then looks back up at you. "I'm glad you're safe."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Hi.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Hi."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You with a dagger in your hand's always a little scary.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Why, does it frighten you?"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Not any--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Boo!"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: (yelps) Okay, it frightens me a little.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She sheathes it on the side.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You okay?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I've had better days. I'm all right."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Good.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "You're alive."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Surprisingly, yes. We won.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I figured, if you're standing here before me. That's good. Congratulations."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Thanks. I'm sorry that I had to go do that. I had to.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I'd be disappointed if you'd've came back with me. More important things."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: So what now? Do you want to go back to Ank'Harel and keep criming?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Thank you for being enough of a shitbag to bring me into it."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Aye, that we did."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: And it's a good sum to get started.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Would it be enough to pay for a right top-end education so I can gloat over all the other kids that are raised by the various lords and ladies of Emon? Become one of those privileged kids."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You can have the best education.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "That's what I want to do. I want to rub it in their face."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right. Education for spite. I like that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "That sounds like a Shorthalt endeavor."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Sure. Whatever you need.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She stands up from the bed and walks over and puts her hand on your shoulder in a pat, pat. She pulls you into a hug and rests her head on your shoulder and doesn't say anything.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You won't have to worry about me anymore.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I'm going to worry about you every day."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Thank you. Thank you for coming into my life.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: She's asleep by the time you get there. Travel and everything has tuckered her out. You tuck her in, put the blanket up.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You won't have to worry about me anymore.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I'm going to worry about you every day."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Thank you. Thank you for coming into my life.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Thank you for being enough of a shitbag to bring me into it."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm so happy. I made so many mistakes, but it turns out that some of them weren't mistakes, after all.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I'm still young and unlearned. Time will tell." She pulls you a little tighter in.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'll snuggle and fall asleep in the room at some point.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She smells the alcohol on your breath and (groans). "Go lie there. I'm going to--" (groans) She winces as her wound in her shoulder flares up. "I need some fresh air. You go to sleep." Exhaustion hits you, and you end up passing out at the foot of the bed.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm in my room now?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, your old room.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to tuck Velora into bed.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You guys make your way into the woods and set up a camp for the evening's rest together. Keyleth, are you in your room? The door (creaks) and Trinket comes in and stares right at you. (slurps) And licks up the side of your face. Your hair sticks up for a minute.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I think that sounds lovely, and you're always better than comfortable. I had a thought, actually. My god, it is hard surviving, isn't it? Perhaps Keyleth could use a friend right now, and I know that you carry one with you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Trinket?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Just for a bit. I think she could use a companion who's not going to apologize or give too much sympathy.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Of course. I send Trinket to Keyleth's room.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As Trinket ascends the stairs, he looks back over the shoulder. (questioning noise)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: She might cry a lot on you. All right, dear?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Makes it up the stairs.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's going to be a lot harder to stay warm out in the woods now.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We'll figure something out. I grew up here. I'm used to it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I wanted to remember how I used to camp with Vax when we were kids.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'd like to see that.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I guess we go out and set up a little fire.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You spend the next two weeks finding your way through the halls of Pandemonium.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All righty, so.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I guess we'll add that to our to do list.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'd like to think that in the next campaign, we're going to find you in a glass coffin like Sleeping fucking Beauty.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: My body's still there.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: She rolled a 17.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: So Grog's body is still there--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He's catatonic until the soul's returned.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Catatonic.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: To be fair, you've sealed Vecna. At level 19 and 20, this is not going to be an earth-shattering experience.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Sure, it's just an annoyance.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And for the sake of the epilogue evening you guys, all planning to return to your various lives to relax--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm there catatonic with an open deck of cards in my hand like this.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You dingus!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Goddamn it!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Somebody's going to find me.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: To Grog! He was the dumbest of us all. He was the dumb and the dumb.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: May he rest in bed sores for all eternity.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: A good moment of, "Oh, we're not going to get a rest." Hopefully, we'll get Ashley back, but in the meantime, for the purposes of this, you come back two weeks after, extremely angry. Grog, as suddenly you're restored, and that tiny bead of light fades back, and you're not outside anymore. You're actually sitting in a reclined, cushioned couch or chair of some kind in Castle Whitestone, and all of your currently living friends are all around you, looking very angry.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Great therapy lesson.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We'll one-shot it later. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: A healing trip.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's a good side quest for the future someday.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Grog's soul was trapped somewhere on the plane of Pandemonium, and you guys had to take two weeks and battle your way through a terrible plane filled with wind and madness and creatures that were banished there to never escape, eventually battling some terrible beast that was guarding the gem that contained his soul, returned, and he just now woke up on the couch after restoring his consciousness to his body, and has been slapped in the face by Keyleth and told that he lost card privileges.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yep. Hand them over.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh, we already took the whole deck.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Wait!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You may lose your belt for this, friend.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. You're lucky we didn't shave your beard. Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I fucked up.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You fucked up a lot.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Percy lost his arm rescuing you!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Are you?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: All right. I just thought, it did fantastic things before! I don't know why it doesn't like me so much.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I don't think it was personal.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Are you sure?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You've learned a lesson that we had to pay for.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: All right. Sorry, guys. Sorry, Pike.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Hello? (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Look at that timing. I don't even know, man.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Pike, can you hear us?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: My internet crashed at the worst fucking time.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We can't even see you right now. You're a silhouette on the screen, but we can hear you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Pike, do you know what happened?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I texted her the gist of what happened.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so you disappeared?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: What does Scanlan do in the next year? Next five years, next ten years?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Oh no.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh no! The internet! Can you hear me, Ashley? (groaning)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Denied.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That was amazing!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That was a yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's 2017. When will we will have better Skype?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That's the most Scanlan thing I could have ever hoped for.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Remember, we're America. We have the worst Internet in the world. Weirdly. Net neutrality. As you guys gather, say your temporary goodbyes, knowing that you're all still very in touch with each other, knowing you care about each other, you begin to forge whatever life looks like outside of perpetual danger and the constant onslaught and struggle of terrors and heroic necessity. Scanlan.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh! Sorry, I'm having a text romance with Ashley Johnson right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Then I'll come back to you.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No. I'm ready. I can pull myself away from Pike for a second.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: It's all right. So assuming that you guys went on a date. I'm discussing with Scanlan his epilogue. What does he wish to do in the next year, five and ten years?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh! Sorry, I'm having a text romance with Ashley Johnson right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Then I'll come back to you.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No. I'm ready. I can pull myself away from Pike for a second.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What does Scanlan do in the next year? Next five years, next ten years?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: (clears throat) I have one prepared. Well, she's not here, so I don't know but I wrote down--

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Can you hear me at all?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh god, I love this!

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Sorry, my Internet keeps going out.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It's okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Perfect timing, Ash.

payload.raw_text: So following off that, the story of Taryon Darrington, which I have something written for here based off what he did. Feel free to embellish or change how you see fit. As the months pass, the Darrington family has adjusted somewhat to a more humble lifestyle, and their work of charity within the city of Deastok and the neighboring lands goes far to clean up the family name from where it once was. While their influence was greatly diminished and they're essentially a political laughingstock, the message of their work still strikes a chord in some of the more philanthropic lords to the north and an alliance is made that protects the Darrington name and interests from the Myriad's ever wiley grasp for the time being. Doty 3.0 now stands a shining golden color, gifted the materials by a thankful ore baron whom Taryon's Brigade of Adventurers saved from a terrifying doppelgänger's plot. His troupe continues to gain a small bit of notoriety in the southern lands of the Dwendalian Empire, some good, some humorous. His mother, Mariya, ever cheering them on and making their tabards for years before passing away peacefully, proud of her boy. Her passing brings the rest of the family closer together than it's ever been. And the charitable efforts, surprisingly, of Howaardt now encompass the Darrington Brigade as a non-profit adventuring league. During a gambling run in Port Demali to follow, Taryon stumbles upon a now slightly more haggard Lawrence, now working as a tanner. They immediately fell right back where they left off and Lawrence cashed in his life as a leather worker to join the Brigade alongside Taryon.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: I thought you were 300 years old. Jesus.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: I mean, that's about it. I think every once in a while, I would maybe try to see if I could see Vax or check in on him somehow, but I don't know how other than to scry.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: Which does not succeed, but you still send your thoughts and wishes into the ether in hopes that they find their way there. Okay.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: So following off that, the story of Taryon Darrington, which I have something written for here based off what he did. Feel free to embellish or change how you see fit. As the months pass, the Darrington family has adjusted somewhat to a more humble lifestyle, and their work of charity within the city of Deastok and the neighboring lands goes far to clean up the family name from where it once was. While their influence was greatly diminished and they're essentially a political laughingstock, the message of their work still strikes a chord in some of the more philanthropic lords to the north and an alliance is made that protects the Darrington name and interests from the Myriad's ever wiley grasp for the time being. Doty 3.0 now stands a shining golden color, gifted the materials by a thankful ore baron whom Taryon's Brigade of Adventurers saved from a terrifying doppelgänger's plot. His troupe continues to gain a small bit of notoriety in the southern lands of the Dwendalian Empire, some good, some humorous. His mother, Mariya, ever cheering them on and making their tabards for years before passing away peacefully, proud of her boy. Her passing brings the rest of the family closer together than it's ever been. And the charitable efforts, surprisingly, of Howaardt now encompass the Darrington Brigade as a non-profit adventuring league. During a gambling run in Port Demali to follow, Taryon stumbles upon a now slightly more haggard Lawrence, now working as a tanner. They immediately fell right back where they left off and Lawrence cashed in his life as a leather worker to join the Brigade alongside Taryon.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: "In the Anus of Dragons," accurately.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: And I hope to finish the book by the time I turn 71.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: How old are you, exactly?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I'd be 70. When we started, I was 69.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You were that young?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You motherfucker!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You're full of shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god, I can't believe you were that young.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: This whole time?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I thought you were 300 years old. Jesus.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Just full of shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I mean, that's about it. I think every once in a while, I would maybe try to see if I could see Vax or check in on him somehow, but I don't know how other than to scry.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Which does not succeed, but you still send your thoughts and wishes into the ether in hopes that they find their way there. Okay.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay. Well you, Keyleth, who many only once saw the naive druid girl have a level of respect now that's carried with that mantle that you hold. And the council of Tal'Dorei occasionally calls upon your wisdom and guidance. The alliance between the civilised and the natured world of the lands irrevocably in Tal'Dorei bound to you at this point, between you and the earth Ashari who reside up in the Cliffkeep. Between occasional jaunts to Emon and Whitestone, you find renewed joy in the protection of the people there. One cold Cuersaar the 13th, the tenth month of the year, the cool summer dusk day closes and you emerge from the quarters to find that the winds come and blow through your hair. You step out to the sound of children laughing as a dozen Ashari kids, elf, dwarf, orc, many bloods and creeds rush past as they play. It's a familiar sound. It's a happy sound. You glance over at this village you're protecting and the people that truly look up to you as their leader, both not just from a might source but as a guide. And you look to them to teach you things every day as well. Looking past as the sun begins to set, you see Korrin standing by the cherry blossom at the edge of the cliff near the amphitheatre where you had your ceremony. In pinks and oranges and purples, the sunset takes the sky. As you approach, he still looks out. He's older, not too much older but older. As he notices you approach, he looks over. "In all my years, not a day passes where awe does not strike me at the beauty of this world." He looks over towards the sunset. "Don't ever lose the memory of every face whose strength helped you keep this light from fading. Every single face."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There is an uptick of ravens in the space of Zephra as well. Not many, but more than there would be, which is usually zero.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: And from the branch of the Sun Tree create my own raven tree of the Ashari. Take a long, long, long, long, long time before I maybe date again. Maybe. But eventually, hopefully, after a long, long, long, long, long time sunset into the tree and become a part of this realm.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's a ways off for you, Ms. Archdruid.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Ways off.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: But you begin those preparations.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: But that's the plan.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay. Well you, Keyleth, who many only once saw the naive druid girl have a level of respect now that's carried with that mantle that you hold. And the council of Tal'Dorei occasionally calls upon your wisdom and guidance. The alliance between the civilised and the natured world of the lands irrevocably in Tal'Dorei bound to you at this point, between you and the earth Ashari who reside up in the Cliffkeep. Between occasional jaunts to Emon and Whitestone, you find renewed joy in the protection of the people there. You step out to the sound of children laughing as a dozen Ashari kids, elf, dwarf, orc, many bloods and creeds rush past as they play. It's a familiar sound. It's a happy sound. You glance over at this village you're protecting and the people that truly look up to you as their leader, both not just from a might source but as a guide. And you look to them to teach you things every day as well. Looking past as the sun begins to set, you see Korrin standing by the cherry blossom at the edge of the cliff near the amphitheatre where you had your ceremony. In pinks and oranges and purples, the sunset takes the sky. As you approach, he still looks out. He's older, not too much older but older. As he notices you approach, he looks over. "In all my years, not a day passes where awe does not strike me at the beauty of this world." He looks over towards the sunset. "Don't ever lose the memory of every face whose strength helped you keep this light from fading. Every single face."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There is an uptick of ravens in the space of Zephra as well. Not many, but more than there would be, which is usually zero.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: And from the branch of the Sun Tree create my own raven tree of the Ashari. Take a long, long, long, long, long time before I maybe date again. Maybe. But eventually, hopefully, after a long, long, long, long, long time sunset into the tree and become a part of this realm.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's a ways off for you, Ms. Archdruid.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Ways off.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: But you begin those preparations.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: But that's the plan.
